{"problem_standardized_math":"Consider the quadratic objective\n\\[\nq(x)=\\frac12 x^T Gx+x^T c,\n\\]\nand suppose that \\(G\\) is positive semidefinite. Assume that \\(x^*\\) satisfies the KKT conditions for the equality-constrained problem\n\\[\n\\min_x\\; q(x)=\\frac12 x^T Gx+x^T c\n\\qquad \\text{subject to} \\qquad\n a_i^T x=b_i, \\quad i\\in \\mathcal{A}(x^*),\n\\]\nfor some multipliers \\(\\lambda_i^*\\), \\(i\\in \\mathcal{A}(x^*)\\). Suppose in addition that the second-order sufficient conditions hold; that is, \\(Z^T G Z\\) is positive definite, where the columns of \\(Z\\) span the null space of the active-constraint Jacobian matrix. Show that \\(x^*\\) is the unique global solution of the original problem, i.e.,\n\\[\nq(x)>q(x^*)\n\\quad \\text{for all feasible } x \\neq x^*.\n\\]"}
